description_long: This object is a vintage-style corded telephone, predominantly colored in pastel lavender with metallic and black accents. The front features a rotary dial with metallic finger holes and a black circular plate, as well as a coin slot and a push button, suggesting it is a payphone. The handset is attached to the left side of the unit via a coiled cord and rests on a cradle. The lower section includes a coin return compartment and a speaker grille, both outlined in black. The overall structure is boxy and robust, with a flat top and a slightly protruding base. The materials appear to be mostly hard plastic and metal, with some glass or acrylic for the dial cover and coin windows.
description: A pastel lavender vintage payphone with a rotary dial, coiled cord handset, and coin slots, featuring metallic and black details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, coin slot, push button, and the handset resting on its cradle.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the handset profile and the thickness of the phone body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back surface and the coiled cord extending from the handset.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the handset cradle and the side profile of the rotary dial and coin compartment.
